title: -Contract: General Code, LLC for Berkeley Municipal Code Publishing Services

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to execute a contract with General
Code, LLC, for online and printed code publishing services for the Berkeley Municipal
Code from November 20, 2025 through November 19, 2030 for a total not to exceed
amount of $75,000.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
The term of the contract with General Code, LLC for code publishing services will be
five years, with $15,000 allocated per year for a total not to exceed amount of $75,000.
Funding for the contract is available in the City Clerk Department’s Fiscal Year 2026
General Fund. Allocations of funds for subsequent years will be subject to adoption of
the Annual Appropriations Ordinance for that fiscal year.
sections.current_situation:
General Code, LLC (General Code), provides the City with code publishing services for
the City Charter and Berkeley Municipal Code (Code). These services include
codification of ordinances into the existing Code, electronic publishing of the Charter
and Code on the City’s website, internet services to support the electronic Code, and
semi-annual full Code reprints. The Code and Charter are presently approximately
2,200 pages. The web platform for the online Code provides for responsive design to
smartly fit the user’s screen size and is compatible with different devices. The web
platform also includes enhanced settings to increase accessibility for individuals with
disabilities, and allows the user to customize the appearance of the site, including fonts,
colors, and how much text is displayed. The navigation and searchability of the Code
allows for robust searching, saving, sharing, bookmarking, and printing, resulting in
enhanced transparency and a user-friendly experience.
The existing contract expires on December 21, 2025 and this new contract with General
Code will allow for continuation of code publishing services through November 19,
2030.

sections.background:
The City Clerk Department is responsible for the processing of ordinances adopted by
the City Council, and ensuring the maintenance and updating of the Code and Charter.
In 2008, staff reviewed proposals for code publishing services, including the full
conversion of the print and electronic Code. Code Publishing Company was selected
and worked with staff to both convert the electronic code to an HTML format online that
allowed users to search, read, and print information more easily, as well as manage the
conversion and reformatting of the hardcopy Code book. The project also entailed on-
going incorporation of adopted ordinances into the Code, including maps, diagrams,
charts, and tables, as well as maintenance of a general alphabetical subject index. In
2021, General Code acquired Code Publishing Company, and General Code continues
to provide code publishing services and fulfill all terms of the existing contract with the
City of Berkeley.
In August 2025, the City released Request For Proposal (RFP) Specification No. 26-
11755 for code publishing services. City staff compiled a list of codification vendors,
and sent each of them notice of the availability of the RFP. One response was
received, and a panel of City staff performed a review of the submitted proposal.
General Code, LLC was selected based on their ability to meet the selection criteria,
including technical ability to perform the service, expertise, references and customer
service, and turnaround time of service provided.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
This contract ensures the continuation of maintenance and support of the electronic
version of the Code on the City’s website. The electronic Code is updated as
ordinances become effective, features robust search capabilities, and provides users
with the option of viewing and printing the Code by title, chapter, or section. The
electronic version of the Code has reduced printing requirements, as users are able to
access the Code from ele
sections.rationale:
General Code has provided the City with a high level of customer service and
competitive pricing. In addition, they provide highly detailed editing and proofreading
services, management of complex Code organization, and offer modern online features
including responsive design for mobile viewing, a customizable interface for improved
accessibility for individuals with disabilities, and advanced searching and navigation
tools for increased efficiency and transparency.
sections.alternatives:
The City Council could consider not approving this contract, however the City does not
currently have in-house resources to update and maintain the Code in the same manner
as a professional codifier. Maintaining the hardcopy and electronic Code requires a
high level of technical and editorial expertise. Staff recommends a contract for this

sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 3

RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT: GENERAL CODE, LLC FOR BERKELEY MUNICIPAL CODE AND CITY
CHARTER PUBLISHING SERVICES
WHEREAS, the City Clerk Department is responsible for the processing of ordinances
adopted by the City Council, and ensuring the maintenance and updating of the
Berkeley Municipal Code and City Charter; and
WHEREAS, General Code, LLC, is the City’s current vendor for code publishing
services, and continues to provide the City with a high level of customer service and
competitive pricing; and
WHEREAS, the existing contract for code publishing services expires on December 21,
2025 and staff recommends a contract for this specialized service; and
WHEREAS, in August 2025, the City released Request For Proposal Specification No.
26-11755 for code publishing services and a panel of City staff selected General Code,
LLC based on their ability to best meet the selection criteria; and
WHEREAS, funds in the amount of $75,000 are allocated in the General Fund budget
for this contract.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager is authorized to execute a contract and any amendments with General
Code, LLC, for Berkeley Municipal Code and City Charter publishing services for the
term of November 20, 2025 through November 19, 2030, for a total not to exceed
amount of $75,000.
